prompt:
Migrate classic-laddie's restic backup config from hand-written NixOS to the-valley's new declarative backup layer. the-valley PR #16 just merged (github:gunk-dev/the-valley, now on main): the CUE schema has an optional host-level 'backup' block (#Backup: enable default true when declared, target 'restic-sftp', cadence 'nightly', retention daily/weekly/monthly defaulting 7/4/6), and the valley-host module renders services.restic.backups.valley from it when the consumer supplies four secret-path options: services.valley.backup.{repositoryFile,passwordFile,sshKeyFile,knownHostsFile}. A NixOS assertion fails the build if backup is declared enabled and any path is unset. Read the merged schema/valley.cue and nix/valley-host.nix on the-valley main for exact semantics before editing.

THE MIGRATION in cosmo:
1. Update the flake lock for the-valley only: nix flake lock --update-input the-valley (must land on a rev including the #Backup schema; verify by inspecting the locked rev's schema/valley.cue).
2. hosts/classic-laddie/valley.cue: add the backup block — target: "restic-sftp" and nothing else (the defaults reproduce today's hand-written policy: nightly, 7/4/6). Keep the file's comment style; note the policy now lives here per the-valley's #Backup.
3. hosts/classic-laddie/default.nix: set services.valley.backup.repositoryFile / passwordFile to the existing agenix secrets (age.secrets.valley-restic-repo / valley-restic-password — move those two age.secrets definitions here from valley-backup.nix, keeping mode 0400), sshKeyFile to the existing valley-git-ssh-key secret path, knownHostsFile to the pinned /var/lib/valley-backup/known_hosts (keep the tmpfiles rule that creates /var/lib/valley-backup, moving it out of valley-backup.nix).
4. DELETE hosts/classic-laddie/valley-backup.nix and its import — but PRESERVE its header runbook: move the human enablement steps (provision box, authorize key, populate the two agenix secrets, pin the box host key) as a comment block next to the new services.valley.backup options, updated to reflect that enablement is now 'declare the backup block in valley.cue' (this PR does that) rather than 'flip cosmo.valley.backup.enable'. The owner is mid-way through populating these exact secrets today — the runbook must stay findable and accurate.
5. Check docs/valley-git-hosting.md and README for references to valley-backup.nix or cosmo.valley.backup.enable; update minimally.

BEHAVIOR NOTES for the PR body: the rendered restic unit should be equivalent to the deleted hand-written one (same timer 03:30+30m Persistent, same prune flags, same sftp identity and pinned known_hosts — diff the rendered unit text against the old config's rendering to prove equivalence, and paste the relevant excerpt in the PR body). Because the block is declared enabled while the secrets may still contain placeholders, the nightly restic run fail-logs harmlessly until the owner finishes populating them — same accepted pattern as the mirror push; say so explicitly.

VERIFY: nix flake check; nix eval .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.build.toplevel.drvPath; render and grep the restic service/timer units from the eval (repository file path, --keep-daily 7, OnCalendar, known_hosts pin); also eval with the backup block removed to confirm no restic units render (sanity of the module wiring). NOTE: another agent is concurrently fixing a desktop-theming build breakage on a different branch (modules/common/desktop.nix) — do not touch that file or attempt a full toplevel BUILD (it currently fails on main for unrelated reasons); eval-level verification plus the unit-text rendering is the gate for this PR.
